description_long: This is a hexagonal brick shelter with a partially open wooden roof frame, revealing the internal support structure. The walls are constructed from light-colored bricks, forming a sturdy base with a regular, geometric pattern. The roof is composed of intersecting wooden beams arranged in a hexagonal pyramid, providing both structural support and partial coverage. The shelter is open on all sides, allowing easy access and visibility throughout the interior. The design suggests it is intended for public use in parks or communal urban spaces, offering shade and protection from light rain while maintaining airflow. The absence of doors or windows and the open framework make it a communal, accessible structure.
description: A hexagonal brick shelter with an open wooden beam roof, providing partial shade and open access on all sides. The structure is sturdy and designed for communal use in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view shows the hexagonal brick base and the open wooden roof frame, with clear visibility into the interior.
description_view_1: This view highlights the symmetry of the hexagonal structure and the intersecting wooden beams of the roof.
description_view_2: Similar to the front, this view emphasizes the open framework and the brick wall pattern.
description_view_3: This angle again displays the hexagonal shape and the open, airy design of the shelter.
attribute_roof_type: hexagonal open wooden beam roof with intersecting rafters
attribute_entryway_design: fully open entryways on all sides with no doors or barriers
attribute_support_structure: brick walls with a wooden beam roof frame
attribute_transparency: partially open roof structure with no transparent panels
